other_observers: The bird was first found with Todd Deininger, then we were joined by Brad Andres, Heather Johnson, Steve Larson, Lisa Edwards, Loch Kirkpatrick, Glenn Walbeck, Joey Kellner and others.; This may have been the same bird reported on 12/19/2011 by Rick and Connie Steinkamp.
specific_location: The bird flew by my car between the middle and eastern dam sections of Jumbo Reservoir then continued flying over the SE bay of the reservoir before perching on a pole along the ridge running south from the campground.
description: Large, heavy, virtually all-white bird which in flight had rounded wings and a slow wing beat. Upon landing the bird showed a rounded head, short tail and a small amount of black barring and spotting and a faint black crown.Behaviors: The bird flew in front of my car at close range and continued on flying with slow heavy wingbeats across a frozen reservoir until reaching the far shore where is swooped up to perch on a wooden utility pole and swivel its head back and forth.Call: NAPlumage: immature
